  "findings": [
    "Torque-to-yield (TTY) bolts reused",
    "Violation of one-time use engineering specification"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Disassemble the joint and discard the reused torque-to-yield bolts",
    "Inspect the female threads and mating surfaces for damage or deformation",
    "Obtain and install brand-new OEM-specified torque-to-yield fasteners",
    "Torque to specification using the correct torque-plus-angle sequence"
  ],
  "rationale": "Torque-to-yield (TTY) fasteners are designed to undergo plastic deformation during installation to achieve their clamping force. Reusing them exceeds their yield strength, leading to potential fatigue failure, thread stripping, or joint relaxation under load.",
  "references": [
    "OEM/engineering specs"
  ]
}
